title: nxusKit Examples Manifest
description: Schema for cross-language example parity manifest (v5.0.0 — adds scenarios array)

examples.description: List of examples that should exist in all implementations

language_display_names.description: Maps machine-facing language keys to human-facing display names (e.g., bash → CLI/Bash)

description.description: Human-readable description of what the example demonstrates

required.description: Whether this example must exist in all implementations

scenario.description: Short phrase describing the scenario demonstrated

real_world_application.description: Short phrase describing the real-world use case

tech_tags.description: Technology tags from the approved tag key

languages.description: Array of implemented languages, must match implementations keys

implementations.description: Paths to implementations in each language

dependencies.description: Other examples this one depends on

tagline.description: Short editorial hook (15-30 words). Used in README blockquote, website cards, Saved Interests context, MCP search results, and CLI output.

blurb.description: Extended editorial paragraph (50-100 words, inverted pyramid structure). Used in MCP detail, future per-example detail pages, and marketing materials.

content_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of inputs used to generate tagline/blurb. Used by CI staleness check.

difficulty.description: Developer skill level required to understand and modify this example.

difficulty_override.description: True if difficulty was manually overridden from the auto-computed value.

difficulty_override_reason.description: Reason for manual difficulty override. Required when difficulty_override is true.

scenarios.description: Named scenarios supported by this example. Omit for single-scenario examples. Each entry has a name, description, and CLI invocation.

name.description: Short scenario identifier (kebab-case, used in CLI flags and display)

description.description: One-line description of what this scenario demonstrates (10-25 words)

cli.description: CLI flag or argument to invoke this scenario (e.g., '--scenario maze-rat')

required.description: Whether examples in this category are required
